comments.text: Natalie Behague has recently found new families of transitive Ramsey sets: [Behague, Natalie. "Nearly all known Euclidean Ramsey sets are subsoluble." arXiv preprint arXiv:2510.15677 (2025), Theorems 1.4 and 1.5]
It is known that Triangles are Ramsey too... by P. Frankl and V. Rodl: All Triangles are Ramsey(1986).
Triangles are special cases of simplices, so they are, in fact, mentioned in the comments.
